weather_report:
Late Sunday morning in Gnarabup opens under an unmarred blue, the kind of clear sky that sharpens every line of dune and reef. The sun already has some bite and will glare more toward early afternoon; expect the UV to climb from manageable to insistent under these cloudless hours, so a hat, sunglasses, and sunscreen will be your quiet companions. The day reads clean and salt-bright, with long views and a calm, uncomplicated mood that invites lingering.

Dress light: a breathable shirt with an easy extra layer for shade, sandals that don’t mind sand, and a bottle of water if you head out. It’s a pleasant day for ordinary joys done well—hang the washing where it can drink the light, take an unhurried amble along the path above the beach, or steal a window seat at the bakery and let the morning saunter by. If you’re drawn to the water, slip in briefly before the midday blaze, then retreat to a patch of shade for a slow lunch and let the afternoon coast along.
